name: Dying Light
description: First-person action survival game set in a post-apocalyptic open world overrun by flesh-hungry zombies. Roam a city devastated by a mysterious virus epidemic. Scavenge for supplies, craft weapons, and face hordes of the infected.
requirements:
MINIMUM:
  • OS *: Windows® 7 64-bit / Windows® 8 64-bit / Windows® 8.1 64-bit
  • Processor: Intel® Core™ i5-2500 @3.3 GHz / AMD FX-8320 @3.5 GHz
  • Memory: 4 GB RAM DDR3
  • Hard Drive: 40 GB free space
  • Graphics: NVIDIA® GeForce® GTX 560 / AMD Radeon™ HD 6870 (1GB VRAM)
  • DirectX®: Version 11
  • Sound: DirectX® compatible
  • Additional Notes: Laptop versions of graphics cards may work but are NOT officially supported.

    Windows-compatible keyboard, mouse, optional controller (Xbox 360 Controller for Windows recommended)
comment1: Hello there. This game was bought for my brother and I, a while ago by my dad. We all enjoyed it alot and then my dad added it to his wishlist. Unfortunatly he passed before we could buy it for him and play with him. I will still be buying it for his account even though he isnt around as a tribute to this good game and all that my dad has done for me in my 19 years of life. Thank you Devs. Miss you dad.
comment2: I generally liked Dying Light, but it didn't really hit me that hard. I enjoyed the story, there are lots of side missions that are fun to do. The characters exist, some moments are interesting, but I can't say the story was very memorable. I mostly just wanted to run around the city, do tasks and fight zombies rather than follow the plot. I liked the gameplay more. Parkour is really cool: you can run on rooftops, jump between buildings and quickly escape from enemies. Fights are fine, there are plenty of weapons, but sometimes it all gets a bit repetitive. Overall, Dying Light is a good game with a fun story. Playing was interesting, I just can't say it really hit me hard.
comment3:
Literally & figuratively one of the greatest zombie games I've ever played. It's a horror & thrill ride masterpiece right until the end. The orange glow of Harran will be forever baked into the back of my mind. Techland started out with 'Dead Island' & it's great, but I always felt like it was missing something. 'Dying Light' is the more sophisticated older brother, in which they cooked to perfection, the successor if you will. From time-to-time in the past as cringe as this is... I would just open up this game just to listen to the main menu theme or even go into game stand on a random rooftop & just listen to the ambience. Everything about this is utter brilliance from the story, soundtrack & even the atmosphere. You won't be disappointed, it's pure nostalgia. In the future when people look back on this game it will be considered a classic in the zombie apocalypse genre. Stop what you're doing & play this from start to finish. It's a must! 

Good Night, Good Luck! My Rating: 9.5/10
